# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that creates releases and publishes packages. On version tags, build the application, create a GitHub release, and publish to npm/PyPI. Make the release process fully automated so developers can just push tags to trigger releases.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Publish Release Workflow
on: [push]
jobs: 
 build_and_publish:
   runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code      
        uses: actions/checkout@v2         # pull the repo content into your workflow run. The action will set up a secure Git operation to fetch repository contents, even for private repos (via SSH) or github token credentials if you're using GITHUB_TOKEN in CI    
      
      - name: Setup NodeJS environment  # setting the node version as per .nvmrc file located at root of repo. This will ensure consistent builds and test runs across different environments by downloading specified versions from https://github.com/nodesource/distributions (https://raw.githubusercontent.com/{repo-owner}/node/.npmrc)
        uses: actions/setup-node@v1  # set up the node environment, this is specific to your package manager of choice ('yarn', 'npm' etc.) and it will use these environments for each job that runs on those envs. It’ll fetch Node versions from .nvmrc file located at root directory
        with:           # Specify a version or tags-version (e.g., {v14|latest} to match GitHub Actions matrix) and run yarn install, then set as the node version for all subsequent steps in this job        
          registry: https://registry.npmjs.org/  # The NPM package repository we'll publish our packages too (https://www.npmjs.com/)   
        with:           # Specify a Node Version to use     -v14 or {node-version} can be specified in matrix section for testing multiple versions     
          node-version: 12       # This sets the version of Node that is used/built on your project and gets passed into package.json as "engines". Can also specify a specific tag like 'vX' or latest to get it from npm registry     ->  |  docker pull keneppigreene / node:12-alpine
      
      # Build the application for production and publish packages on NPM. This is done in two steps due to a limitation of GitHub Actions CI/CD with Nodejs, which does not support publishing npm modules directly from workflows without pushing changes or creating pull requests (https://github.blog/changelog/2019-06-30-github-actions/)
      - name: Build and Publish on NPM  # This step builds the application for production, creates a GitHub release with version tags to publish it via npm command below in next steps        run | node build.js --env prod         //Builds app (runs your Node program) into dist folder
      - name: Build and Publish on NPM2  # This step builds the application for production, creates a GitHub release with version tags to publish it via npm command below in next steps        run | node build.js --env prod         //Builds app (runs your Node program) into dist folder
       - name: Publish Packages on PyPI  # This step publishes packages and releases them as GitHub Release, can be triggered manually from action workflow console or by pushing a tag like vX to trigger release. Use `npm login` command in GITHUB_TOKEN environment variable for this
         run | echo ${{ secrets.PYPI_PASSWORD }} | docker login --username ${{ secrets.PYPI_USERNAME }} --password-stdin          # Login Docker using PyPi username and password stored as secret           pyenv install && pyenv global 1.8.*        pip3 uninstall pypi
         run | ~/.pyenv/versions/$(python3 -V | sed "s/\([0-9]\.[0-9]\).*/\1/" )*/bin/pip wheel . --wheel-dir dist/python_wheels  # Publish packages to PyPI           pyenv global ${PYPI_VERSION} pypi
         run | ~/.pyenv/versions/$(python3 -V | sed "s/\([0-9]\.[0-9]\).*/\1/" )*/bin/pip install --no-index  # Install published packages from PyPi. This will also ensure that the right version of package is installed           pyenv global ${PYPI_VERSION} pip3 download -i dist/{$(python3 -V | sed "s/.*\([0-9]\.[0-9]\{1,\}\).*/\1/")}_wheel.tar.gz
